dependencies: Third-party property owners for management and franchise contracts
Developers and owners ability to access capital for development, maintenance and renovation of properties
Third-party service providers for operational and technical services
Third-party internet and travel intermediaries who sell hotel rooms
risks: Financial condition of third-party property owners, developers and joint venture partners
Owners may terminate or fail to comply with management, franchise, joint venture or strategic partner contracts
Delays in or cancellations of planned or future development or refurbishment projects
Shortages of desirable locations for development
Dependence on third parties for operational and technical services
Third-party security incidents could expose company to liability
Delays in service from third-party service providers

regulatory_risks: Foreign and U.S. federal and state laws and regulations governing hospitality properties
Licensing and regulation by health, sanitation, fire and safety standards
Laws governing relationships with employees including minimum wage requirements, overtime, working conditions and citizenship requirements
Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) compliance requirements
Changes in U.S. federal, state and local or foreign tax law
Environmental, health and safety laws and regulations
Laws intended to lessen the impact of climate change
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) compliance
Trade sanctions administered by Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC)
U.K. Bribery Act 2010 and local anti-corruption laws
Modern slavery, human trafficking and forced labor reporting and due diligence laws (U.K. Modern Slavery Act 2015, German Supply Chain Due Diligence Act)
Data protection, cybersecurity and privacy laws (GDPR, California Privacy Rights Act, CAN-SPAM Act, Telephone Consumer Protection Act)
Marketing and advertising laws including direct marketing regulations
Casino gaming licensing and regulatory control
Terrorism Risk Insurance Program expiration risks
Collective bargaining agreements and labor regulations
Franchise laws and regulations at federal and state levels
Building and zoning requirements
Alcoholic beverage service regulations
Food preparation and sale regulations
Sustainability reporting regulations (European Union Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive)

sanction_risks: OFAC sanctions compliance - hotels may provide services to or receive funds from persons subject to sanctions
Voluntary disclosures to OFAC for potential violations identified
Trade sanctions and embargoes affecting international operations
